data.title: Tesla's deliveries report is called a 'disaster' by Wedbush as sell-side confidence fades
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) fell on Wednesday after posting its worst quarterly deliveries number since 2022. Expectations for Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Q1 earnings report had already been dramatically reduced, but the tally of just 336K deliveries for the quarter was still below the consensus estimate.

In general, Wall Street analysts are bracing for more negative news, with very few "buy the dip" recommendations out there.

RBC analyst Tom Narayan advised that the weak quarter was not a total surprise and already baked in to Tesla's (TSLA) share price to a degree already. "What we might have gotten wrong was expecting a boost to March numbers given a pull forward expectation in the US ahead of potential tariffs," he noted.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said the numbers were a "disaster" on every metric. "Musk needs to stop this political firestorm and balance being CEO of Tesla with DOGE," warned Ives on the self-inflicted crisis. 

Hargreaves Lansdown analyst Matt Britzman said there was no way to sugarcoat the report. "Tesla’s first-quarter delivery numbers are a disappointment, though many investors were already preparing for a soft number. A drop from last year is no surprise, but the scale is worse than many had expected," he wrote. 

Meanwhile, Barclays warned that the soft Q1 deliveries print from Tesla (TSLA) creates a challenging path ahead. For its part, Oppenheimer said it will remain cautious on Tesla (TSLA) after digesting the big decline in deliveries.

Looking ahead, Future Fund Active ETF co-founder and Tesla (TSLA) shareholder Gary Black highlighted that the results will push analysts to reduce full-year 2025 delivery estimates and earnings estimates. Of course, that would also impact the trading multiple, which is already considered elevated by many analysts, even after factoring in the Cybercab, Optimus humanoid robot, FSD licensing, and various AI initiatives.

On Seeking Alpha, analyst Kevin Shan warned on Wednesday that robotaxis are not the answer for Tesla's (TSLA) long-term challenges. "Self-driving and robotics no doubt have potential, but it's a market where Tesla is competing against massive tech companies with healthier core businesses and has already begun generating revenue from their developments," he wrote.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were down 2.1% in morning trading on Wednesday to $262.80.

data.title: Raymond James revises airline ratings amid market uncertainty, upgrades Allegiant
data.description:
Ahead of quarterly results from the major U.S. carriers, Raymond James adjusted their outlook on certain carriers to reflect the elevated macro uncertainty in the airline industry, including weakness in off-peak leisure travel and the loss of momentum for corporate demand.

Among those singled out is United Airlines Holdings (NASDAQ:UAL) which was downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform. While Raymond James analyst Savanthi Syth sees the recent pullback in United (NASDAQ:UAL) stock as an overcorrection relative to the current environment where premium and long-haul international demand remain solid, United (NASDAQ:UAL) faces greater earnings risk should these segments start to falter. Instead, Syth prefers Delta Air Lines (DAL), which she rates as a Strong Buy.

Syth also gives high marks to Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) which she upgrades to Strong Buy from Outperform given Allegiant’s (ALGT) "idiosyncratic earnings drivers" that should help offset any macro-driven headwinds. This also applies to Outperform-rated American Airlines Group (AAL), and Southwest Airlines (LUV).

While she sees better risk-reward elsewhere, Syth believes JetBlue Airways (JBLU) offers an “interesting opportunity for those looking to be nimble in the near-term due to extreme bearishness present in the stock.” Syth views JetBlue (JBLU) as a Market Perform airline stock.

After a string of losses reaching as many as six consecutive days in the red, airline stocks are modestly higher with the exception of Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) up 6% on Tuesday’s upgrade.

In related news, United Airlines (UAL) becomes the first U.S. carrier to offer flights to Bangkok, Thailand, and Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam with daily service beginning in October from Hong Kong.

Also beginning in October, United (UAL) will add a second daily flight between San Francisco and Manila, making the carrier the only U.S. airline to serve the Philippines. The carrier is also adding non-stop flights between the U.S. and Adelaide, Australia, complementing flights to Sydney, Brisbane, and Melbourne.

data.title: AngioDynamics targets 14%-16% MedTech growth for fiscal 2025 driven by thrombectomy and NanoKnife adoption
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AngioDynamics (ANGO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jim Clemmer highlighted strong Q3 performance with total revenue reaching $72 million, reflecting over 9% year-over-year growth. MedTech revenue grew by 22%, with continued success across platforms.
  • Clemmer emphasized the performance of the Auryon platform, which grew 17% year-over-year, and the Mechanical Thrombectomy portfolio, including AlphaVac and AngioVac, which combined grew 47%. AlphaVac alone achieved 160% growth.
  • NanoKnife adoption was bolstered by its 16% increase in disposable revenue. The FDA’s expanded indication for NanoKnife in prostate tissue ablation, combined with new CPT codes effective January 2026, positions the product for long-term growth.
  • CFO Stephen Trowbridge noted Q3 adjusted EBITDA of $1.3 million, a turnaround from a loss in the same quarter of 2024, and gross margins improved to 54%.

Outlook

  • Fiscal 2025 revenue guidance was raised to $285-$288 million, with MedTech growth expected in the 14%-16% range.
  • Gross margin guidance increased to 53%-54%, up from the previous 52%-53% estimate.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to $4-$5 million, and adjusted loss per share is expected to range from $0.31 to $0.34.
  • Management expects continued sequential and year-over-year growth in thrombectomy and NanoKnife adoption, particularly as they expand reimbursement and coverage for prostate indications.

Financial Results

  • MedTech revenue reached $31.3 million, contributing 44% of total revenue, compared to 39% in Q3 2024.
  • Auryon revenue was $13.9 million, achieving double-digit growth for the 15th consecutive quarter.
  • AlphaVac revenue grew to $3 million, a 161% year-over-year increase, while AngioVac revenue reached $6.8 million, up 23%.
  • NanoKnife revenue totaled $6.3 million, supported by a 16% rise in probe revenue.
  • The Med Device segment generated $40.7 million, achieving a 1% year-over-year increase.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $44.8 million as of February 28, 2025. The company expects cash generation in Q4, ending the year with approximately $55 million.

Q&A

  • John Young, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about the sustainability of AngioVac’s performance. CFO Trowbridge confirmed that AngioVac’s Q3 performance of $6.8 million is a solid baseline and expects continued growth driven by the synergistic portfolio of AngioVac and AlphaVac.
  • Eduardo Martinez, H.C. Wainwright: Inquired about the AMBITION BTK trial for Auryon. Trowbridge stated the trial will evaluate outcomes for treating critical limb ischemia and aims to demonstrate Auryon’s efficacy in below-the-knee procedures.
  • Steven Lichtman, Oppenheimer: Asked about Auryon’s European market opportunities. CEO Clemmer noted extensive education and awareness campaigns, expecting revenue contributions from Q4.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment about growth drivers like Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife, while seeking clarity on market penetration and reimbursement timelines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong execution and strategic positioning in high-growth markets.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ tone showed increased optimism about sustained growth in key product segments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue remained consistent, with Q3 revenue at $72 million compared to Q2’s $73 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA declined slightly from $3.1 million in Q2 to $1.3 million in Q3, attributed to sequential investments.
  • MedTech growth continued to outpace Med Device, with a notable rise in AlphaVac and NanoKnife performance.
  • Guidance was raised for revenue, gross margin, and adjusted EBITDA, reflecting management’s confidence in execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential challenges related to reimbursement adoption for NanoKnife’s prostate indications, emphasizing ongoing efforts to secure payer support by the effective date of the new CPT codes.
  • Analysts raised concerns over the pace of adoption in new markets, particularly in Europe for Auryon and AlphaVac.
  • Tariff impacts were noted as potential risks, but management does not expect material effects due to a predominantly U.S.-based supply chain.

Final Takeaway

AngioDynamics delivered strong Q3 results, highlighting growth across its MedTech portfolio. With raised guidance and increasing adoption in key areas like Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife, the company is positioned for sustained growth. Management’s confidence is underscored by continued investments in clinical data and market expansion, while addressing reimbursement challenges and leveraging synergies across its product lines.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BioMarin succeeds in trial for its metabolic disorder treatment
data.description:
• BioMarin Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:BMRN) said on Wednesday that its late-stage trial of PALYNZIQ, a treatment for phenylketonuria, met main goal.
  • The study led to a statistically significant lowering in blood Phe levels in adolescents aged 12-17 with phenylketonuria, a metabolic disorder that prevents the body from breaking down the amino acid phenylalanine, compared to diet alone.
  • The company  said that when it came to safety, results were consistent with the known profile of the medicine.
  • Biomarin plans to submit for regulatory approval of PALYNZIQ later this year.

data.title: Fosun International reports FY results
data.description:
• Fosun International press release (OTCPK:FOSUY): FY total revenue of RMB192.14 billion, with industrial operating profit, a key indicator for corporate operational performance, reaching RMB4.9 billion; overseas revenue proportion increased from 45% to 49.3%.
  • Loss attributable to owners of the parent amounted to approximately RMB4.35 billion, mainly due to a non-cash impairment loss related to the Cainiao investment.

data.title: S&P 500 Industrial ends almost flat in Q1, Airlines drag index lower
data.description:
The Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLI), which tracks S&P 500 Industrial Index (SP500-20) closed almost flat, down just 0.52% at the end of Q1, while the broader index S&P500 ETF (SPY) had a bad start to 2025, crashing 4.55% at the end of Q1.

Despite overall market difficulties, eight S&P sectors remain positive for Q1, with the Energy Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF leading the way.

Industries Q1 Performance

Top 5 movers of Q1:

Gainers:

Republic Services (RSG) +20.4%. 

GE Aerospace (GE) +20.1%.

Howmet Aerospace (HWM) +18.5%. 

Waste Management (WM) +15.7%.

RTX (RTX) +15%.

Losers:

United Airlines (UAL) -31.78%.

Delta Airlines (DAL) -30.6%.

Hubbell (HUBB) -21.1%.

Quanta (PWR) -19.5%.

Generac Holdings (GNRC) -18.9%.

Deteriorating consumer sentiment, fears of a return of problematic inflation, and concerns surrounding a boycott of U.S. tourist destinations weighed on the airline sector, causing shares of major players in the sector to lose altitude in Q1.

What Quantitative Measures say?

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating has given XLI a BUY rating, with a score of 3.67 out of 5. The rating system has given the industrial sector tracking fund an A+ in liquidity and expenses, A in dividends, B in momentum and a D in risk. 

Fund Flows

The Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF recorded an outflow of $506.28M in the first quart

er.

source: etfdb.com

data.title: Atlassian upgraded to Overweight following first quarter pullback: Cantor
data.description:
Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by Cantor Fitzgerald due in part to the pullback in share price during the first quarter of 2025.

Shares for the Sydney-based provider of project management and team collaboration software company ticked up about 2% during early Wednesday market action.

Cantor also lowered its price target on the stock to $272 from $304.

"Software has underperformed YTD C25 with the IGV (BATS:IGV) software benchmark down 10% compared to -4% for the broader SPX (SPX), while we note the tech-heavy Nasdaq composite is also down 10% YTD," said Cantor Fitzgerald analysts, led by Thomas Blakey, in a Wednesday investor note. "We note SOX (SOX) is down 14% YTD, as the Al-driven momentum trade has stalled here in early 2025. Software EV/R multiples have contracted 12% YTD, with the high-growth (20%+) cohort multiple contracting 8%."

Cantor lowered their price targets for a wide range of infrastructure and artificial intelligence software names in their coverage universe:

  • Datadog (DDOG) reduced to $134 from $161; Overweight rating
  • DigitalOcean (DOCN) to $38 from $43; Neutral
  • Five9 (FIVN) to $36 from $57; Overweight
  • MongoDB (MDB) to $252 from $332; Overweight
  • monday.com (MNDY) to $336 from $380; Overweight
  • Cloudflare (NET) to $120 from $149; Neutral
  • Snowflake (SNOW) to $183 from $228; Overweight
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN) to $8.50 from $10; Neutral

data.title: Factory orders rise slightly more than expected in February
data.description: U.S. factory orders drifted up 0.6% M/M to $594.0B, slightly stronger than the 0.5% increase expected but decelerating from January's 1.8% rise (revised from +1.7%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday.

data.title: Apollo Asset Management: Corporate confidence takes a hit as execs signal concern
data.description:
Apollo Asset Management has observed a noticeable decline in corporate confidence among CEOs and CFOs over the past few months, as Wall Street grapples with uncertainty and strives to regain its footing. This comes amid ongoing tariff negotiations, growing fears of a potential recession, and the S&P 500's recent slip into correction territory.

Torsten Sløk, Apollo’s chief economist, highlighted this shift, noting, “Surveys of CEOs and CFOs show that corporate confidence has declined in recent months.” 

This decline in corporate confidence coincides with a broader economic concern, as consumer confidence also saw a dip for the fourth consecutive month in March. The U.S. Consumer Confidence Index fell to 92.9, falling short of the expected 94.2 and the previous month's figure of 94.2, signaling further cracks in the economic outlook. 

Below are two visual charts provided by Apollo Asset Management, which further illustrate these trends:

For investors looking to keep a broader pulse on the market see below some index and fixed income focused exchange traded funds:

Market tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ).

data.title: FTI Consulting cut to Hold at Truist on risk emanating from Economic Consulting segment
data.description:
Shares of FTI Consulting (NYSE:FCN) were downgraded to Hold from Buy at Truist Securities, which also cut its price target on the business advisory firm to $178.00 from $225.00.

The capital markets company listed ongoing risk to estimates from consultant attrition in FCN's Economic Consulting segment, risk to less regulatory-driven demand under a Trump administration, and dearth of M&A as its reasons for the drop in rating.

Analysts at Truist lowered their 2025 estimates and now expected $3.647B in revenue from $3.725B in 2025 versus the $3.740M consensus, adj. EBITDA to $405M from $416M previously and the $420M consensus, and adj. EPS to $7.74 from $7.98 vs. the $7.91 consensus.

Meanwhile, for 2026, estimated sales were $3.839M vs. the $4.005B consensus, adj. EBITDA of $441M vs. $466M and adj. EPS of $8.62 vs. $8.97.

"We believe that FTI can emerge from the Economic Consulting headwinds and regain substantial positive organic revenue, EBITDA, and EPS growth," said Truist while adding that clarity around this could take months or quarters.

Since the start of the year, FTI Consulting has fallen about 16.3%. Seeking Alpha's Quant rated the company as a Sell while Wall Street remained a Buy on the stock.
